Submodules

flashy.models.batch_pulses module

class flashy.models.batch_pulses.BatchPulses(analysis_level_method, analysis_area_calc_method, analysis_nC2cGy_factor, digitizer_sampeling_period_ns, digitizer_ADC2V_factor, detector_Vns2nC_factor, pulses, raw_valid_pulses=array([], shape=(1, 0), dtype=float64), area_under_curves=array([], dtype=float64), doses=array([], dtype=float64), discard_flag=False)

Bases: Struct

Container holding a batch of processed and raw pulse data along with associated analysis results.

Inherits:

msgspec.Struct

analysis_level_method: str

Method used for pulse level reconstruction or processing.

analysis_area_calc_method: str

Method used to compute pulse area under the curve.

analysis_nC2cGy_factor: float

Conversion factor from nC to cGy for dose calculation.

digitizer_sampeling_period_ns: float

Sampling period of the digitizer in nanoseconds.

digitizer_ADC2V_factor: float

Conversion factor from ADC units to volts.

detector_Vns2nC_factor: float

Conversion factor from V·ns to nC for detector response.

pulses: ndarray

Processed pulse data (leveled or reconstructed signals).

raw_valid_pulses: ndarray

Raw pulse data after validity filtering.

area_under_curves: ndarray

Computed area under each pulse curve.

doses: ndarray

Dose values computed from pulses.

discard_flag: bool

Flag indicating whether this batch should be ignored in analysis.

property has_pulses: bool

Check whether the batch contains any pulses.

Returns:

True if at least one pulse is present.

Return type:

bool

add_pulses(pulses, raw_pulses, areas, doses)

Append new pulse data to the current batch.

All arrays are concatenated along their first axis (or flattened where appropriate).

Parameters:

  • pulses (np.ndarray) – Processed pulses to append.

  • raw_pulses (np.ndarray) – Raw pulse data to append.

  • areas (np.ndarray) – Pulse area values to append.

  • doses (np.ndarray) – Dose values to append.

Returns:

None

Return type:

None

flashy.models.processing_config module

class flashy.models.processing_config.AcquisitionConfig(digitizer, detector_assignments)

Bases: Struct

Parameters for an acquisition configuration.

Example:

AcquisitionConfig(
    digitizer=caen,
    detector_assignments=[
        DetectorAssignment(
            detector=bergoz_1,
            digitizer_channel=0,
        ),
        DetectorAssignment(
            detector=bergoz_2,
            digitizer_channel=1,
        ),
    ]
)
Inherits:

msgspec.Struct

digitizer: CaenDT5781Config

Used digitizer during acquisition.

detector_assignments: list[DetectorAssignment]

List of detectors where each element represents a channel of the digitizer.

class flashy.models.processing_config.ProcessingConfig(acquisition, analysis)

Bases: Struct

Parameters for processing pulses with acquisition and analysis configurations.

Inherits:

msgspec.Struct

acquisition: AcquisitionConfig

The acquisition configuration used for processing.

analysis: AnalysisConfig

The analysis configuration used for processing.

flashy.models.user_config module

class flashy.models.user_config.UserConfig(project_path='', name_of_shoot='', increment_name='', path_of_shoot='', increment=1, path_to_logs='logs')

Bases: Struct

User configuration container.

Inherits:

msgspec.Struct

project_path: str

Root project path used for loading/saving data.

name_of_shoot: str

Name of the acquisition session or “shoot”.

increment_name: str

Suffix used when generating incremented file or run names.

path_of_shoot: str

Full path where the current shoot data is stored.

increment: int

Increment index used for run/versioning.

path_to_logs: Literal['logs']

Relative or fixed path where logs are stored.

increment_name_of_shoot()

Increment the shoot name and create the corresponding directory.

Raises:

FileExistsError – If the target directory already exists.

set_name_of_shoot(name)

Set a new shoot name and initialize its directory.

Parameters:

name (str) – New shoot name.

Raises:

FileExistsError – If a directory for this shoot already exists.
